info.title: meter engine API
info.description: The metering engine's HTTP surface — money-truth and usage. The source of truth for engine SDK codegen.

get.summary: `GET /health` — liveness; static "the process is up and serving".
get.description:
Deliberately independent of the stores, so a transient database blip never trips a liveness probe
into a restart loop.

200.description: The process is up

get.summary: `GET /health/ready` — readiness; pings both stores and reports which is down.
get.description:
`200` only when the money store (Postgres) and the event store (ClickHouse) both answer, otherwise
`503`. This is the probe a load balancer / k8s should gate traffic on.

200.description: Both stores reachable
Account opened
Account balance (settled / held)
Usage vs limit with threshold status

post.summary: `POST /v1/accounts/{id}/credit-notes` — credit an account back (a refund / correction).

200.description: Refund posted; returns the ledger entry
The account's ledger entries (audit trail)
The account's latest (non-voided) events
Grant posted; returns the ledger entry
Invoice summed from the ledger over the period
Daily credit totals over the period

get.summary: `GET /v1/audit` — newest-first, optionally filtered by actor, method, and time window.

200.description: Matching audit entries, newest first

get.description:
Prices are best-effort as of `as_of` (no billing-accuracy SLA); verify against the provider before
billing.

200.description: Snapshot date + every catalogued model's per-token prices

get.summary: `GET /v1/catalog/{model_id}` — the provider-cost rate card for a catalogued model.
get.description: Ready to price usage against. `404` if the model is not in the catalog.

200.description: The provider-cost rate card
Event recorded (idempotent on org + key)

post.summary: `POST /v1/events/batch` — bulk ingest; returns `202 Accepted` with the count recorded.
post.description:
Ids are content-addressed from `(org_id, idempotency_key)`, so callers can derive them without the
payload.

202.description: Batch accepted; returns the count recorded

200.description: The event
New event version superseding the prior
Lease (session sub-account) opened
Unused remainder returned to the parent

get.summary: `GET /v1/orgs/{id}/event-count` — count of an organization's live (recorded) events.

200.description: Count of live (recorded) events

get.summary: `GET /v1/orgs/{id}/reconcile` — reconcile every pre-aggregated rollup (by model, and each promoted
get.description:
custom field) against the event store of record. An empty array means the fast read paths agree with
ground truth; any returned row is drift (scope + dimension) that warrants rebuilding that rollup.

200.description: Per-group rollup-vs-source drift; empty means consistent

get.summary: `GET /v1/orgs/{id}/usage-by-day` — daily event + credit totals from the event store (`ClickHouse`).

200.description: Daily event + credit totals

get.summary: `GET /v1/orgs/{id}/usage-by-field?field=<name>` — flexible credit burndown grouped by any factor.

parameters.name: field
parameters.description: Factor to group by: a custom event field (e.g. team, feature, customer) or a built-in like model

200.description: Credit burndown per value of the factor, highest spend first

get.summary: `GET /v1/orgs/{id}/usage-by-model` — usage aggregated by model, ordered by spend.
get.description: Derived from the event store (`ClickHouse`); reflects amends and voids; highest spend first.

200.description: Usage aggregated by model, ordered by spend

get.summary: `GET /v1/rate-cards` — every synced rate card (live version each).

200.description: Every synced rate card (live version each)

get.summary: `GET /v1/rate-cards/{id}` — the live (latest-version) synced rate card; `404` if none was synced.

200.description: The live synced rate card

404.description: No card synced for this id

200.description: Reservation outcome (allowed or denied)

post.summary: `POST /v1/reservations/{id}/extend` — push the hold's expiry forward (heartbeat keep-alive).

200.description: Settle posted; returns the ledger entry

post.summary: `POST /v1/runs/{id}/void` — kill a whole run: void its events and reverse its ledger impact.
post.description:
Voids the run's events (append-only: each is marked voided) and reverses its ledger impact (release
open holds, refund settled charges). Both halves are idempotent, so retrying a void is safe.

200.description: Counts of events voided + ledger holds/charges reversed
Credit totals for both models and the delta
Priced, recorded, and charged; returns credits + balance

post.summary: `POST /v1/usage/reservations/{id}/settle` — price the actual usage and settle the reservation.
post.description:
Prices the actuals against a catalog model and closes the reservation. Idempotent on the
reservation id.

200.description: Credits charged + balance after

post.summary: `POST /v1/usage/reserve` — price a worst-case estimate against a catalog model and place a hold.
post.description: The engine computes the credits (ADR 0001). Returns the reserve outcome plus the reserved credits.

200.description: Reserve outcome plus the reserved credits

AccountScope.description: What a ledger account represents within the org hierarchy and the credit machinery.

idempotency_key.description:
Optional idempotency key: a retried amend with the same key resolves to the same new version
instead of stacking a second one.

properties.description: Replacement customer JSON (the amend records a new version).

Balance.description: A point-in-time balance: settled credits, and credits currently held by open reservations.

held.description: Credits currently locked by open reservations, as an exact decimal string.

settled.description: Settled credit balance, as an exact decimal string.

BudgetStatusResponse.description:
Usage in a period against a limit, with threshold classification. Credit amounts are exact decimal
strings.

package.description: Bundled pricing: charge `unit_price` per `size`-unit package, rounding the quantity up.

ChargeModel.description:
How a component computes its charge from a quantity.

- [`Standard`](ChargeModel::Standard): flat per-unit pricing (the component's `unit_price`).
- [`Graduated`](ChargeModel::Graduated): each tier's price applies only to the units that fall in
that tier's band (like marginal tax brackets).
- [`Volume`](ChargeModel::Volume): the single tier the *total* quantity lands in prices every unit.
- [`Package`](ChargeModel::Package): units are sold in fixed-size bundles — the quantity is rounded
*up* to a whole number of packages, each charged at the component's `unit_price` (e.g. "$0.01 per
1000 tokens, any partial 1000 rounds up").

Tiered schedules must be ascending by `up_to` and end with an unbounded [`PriceTier::rest`] tier;
otherwise pricing returns [`PricingError::InvalidTierSchedule`]. A `Package` size must be positive
or pricing returns [`PricingError::InvalidPackageSize`].

ContextTier.description: A context-length tier (long-context tokens are often priced higher).

CreditSource.description:
Provenance of credits, carried onto every entry so revenue recognition can split real product
margin from promotional spend.

Currency.description: An ISO-4217-style alphabetic currency code (three uppercase ASCII letters, e.g. `USD`).

DayUsage.description: Credits consumed on a single UTC day — a point in a usage time series.

total_credits.description: Total credits consumed that day, as an exact decimal string.

EntryType.description: The kind of a ledger posting.

Event.description:
One usage event. `properties` is arbitrary customer JSON; `run_id` groups the events of one agent
run. Amendments create a new event whose `supersedes` points back to the version it replaces.

`Event` is not `Eq` because `properties` (a `serde_json::Value`) is only `PartialEq`.

EventCountResponse.description: The count of an organization's live (recorded) events.

EventDayUsage.description: Daily usage totals for an organization (a time series for charts). `day` is `YYYY-MM-DD`.

EventStatus.description: The lifecycle status of an event version.

FieldUsage.description:
Credit burndown for one value of an arbitrary grouping factor — a custom event field (e.g.
`team`, `feature`, `customer`) or a built-in like `model`. `dimension` is the field's value;
`credits` is the burnable spend attributed to it (events that recorded no credits contribute 0,
so non-burnable usage shows up as `events > 0` with `credits = 0`).

amount.description: Credit amount as an exact decimal string.

InvoiceResponse.description: A period invoice: total credits and the entry count, summed from the ledger (enforced == billed).

entries.description: Number of spend postings in the period.

total_credits.description: Total credits consumed over the period, as an exact decimal string.

LedgerAccount.description: A node in the ledger that carries a credit balance, optionally leased from a parent account.

no_overdraft.description: When true, a HARD reservation can never drive available credits negative.

org_id.description: The tenant that owns this account ([`SYSTEM_ORG`] for the system account).

parent_id.description: The parent pool this account leases from, if it is a session sub-balance.

LedgerEntry.description:
One immutable transfer between two accounts. Never updated after creation; corrections are new
entries that point back via [`LedgerEntry::reverses_entry_id`].

account_id.description: The account whose balance moves by `delta_credits`.

balance_after.description: `account_id`'s settled balance immediately after this entry (stored so audits never replay).

delta_credits.description: Signed change to `account_id`'s settled balance, as an exact decimal string.

idempotency_key.description: The client idempotency key that produced this entry, when applicable.

paired_account_id.description: The counter-account the equal-and-opposite delta posts to (double-entry).

reservation_id.description: The reservation this entry belongs to, when part of a reserve→settle flow.

revenue_recognizable.description: Whether the credits on this entry are recognizable revenue.

reverses_entry_id.description: For reversals/amendments: the entry this one corrects.

Margin.description:
A markup multiplier applied to provider cost to get the customer price. `1.30` is a 30% markup;
[`Margin::NONE`] (`1.00`) charges cost.

MeterUsageBody.description: `POST /v1/usage` — price token usage via the catalog, record the event, and charge credits.

burnable.description:
Whether this usage burns credits. `true` (the default) prices and debits the ledger as usual.
`false` records the event with its real provider cost for visibility but never touches the
ledger — its burned `credits` are zero, so burndown stays truthful while you keep flexible,
non-charging usage (free tiers, internal traffic, trials). The would-be charge is preserved in
the event's `priced_credits` property.

rate_card_id.description: Optional synced rate-card id to price against; defaults to the catalog card for `model`.

tags.description:
Optional custom fields recorded on the event for flexible credit burndown (e.g.
`{"team":"alpha","feature":"chat"}`). Reserved keys (model, the token dimensions, credits, …)
can't be overridden. Slice spend by any of these via `GET /v1/orgs/{id}/usage-by-field`.

MeterUsageResult.description:
`POST /v1/usage` result: the priced amounts, the recorded event id, and the resulting balance.
Credit/USD amounts are exact decimal strings.

credits.description: Credits actually burned (debited from the ledger). Zero for non-burnable usage.

priced_credits.description: What the usage priced to, whether or not it burned. Equals `credits` for burnable usage.

Modality.description: The modality of the priced content.

ModelUsage.description: Usage aggregated by model for one organization.

Money.description: A monetary amount in a specific currency.

amount.description: The amount as an exact decimal string.

PriceComponent.description: One priced cell: a (dimension, modality, context-tier) charged at `unit_price` per `unit`.

PriceTier.description:
One band of a tiered charge: it covers quantity up to `up_to` cumulative units (inclusive), or is
the final unbounded band when `up_to` is `None`. The band's units are charged at `unit_price`.

up_to.description: Inclusive cumulative upper bound of this band in units; `None` means unbounded (the last tier).

PricingDimension.description: A priceable dimension of an LLM/agent usage event.

RateCard.description: A versioned rate card: one card, many priced dimensions, one margin.

RateCardKind.description: Whether a card records provider cost (COGS) or what a customer is charged.

RecordBatchBody.description:
`POST /v1/events/batch` — the firehose ingest path: many events in one round-trip, written in a
single bulk insert. Idempotent per event on `(org_id, idempotency_key)`, exactly like `record`.

properties.description: Arbitrary customer JSON describing the usage.

RefundBody.description: `POST /v1/accounts/{id}/credit-notes` — credit an account back (a refund / correction).

amount.description: Credit amount as an exact decimal string.

reverses_entry_id.description: The original ledger entry being reversed (UUID), if known.

expires_at.description: Optional hold expiry (RFC3339); an open hold past it is released by the sweep.

run_id.description: Optional agent run this hold belongs to; lets `POST /v1/runs/{id}/void` reverse it.

oneOf.description: The hold was placed (or already existed); the caller may proceed to spend.
A HARD limit refused the spend; the call must not proceed.

ReserveUsageBody.description:
`POST /v1/usage/reserve` — price a worst-case usage estimate against a catalog model and place a
hold before the call. The engine computes the credits (ADR 0001), not the caller.

rate_card_id.description: Optional synced rate-card id to price against; defaults to the catalog card for `model`.

run_id.description: Optional agent run this hold belongs to; lets `POST /v1/runs/{id}/void` reverse it.

reserved_credits.description: Credits the engine priced and reserved, as an exact decimal string.

ReserveUsageResult.description:
`POST /v1/usage/reserve` result: the reserve outcome (flattened) plus the engine-computed credits
that were held.

RollupDrift.description:
One group's drift between a pre-aggregated rollup and the live `events` system of record. `scope` is
which rollup (`model`, or a promoted custom field like `team`) and `dimension` is the group value.
Returned only when the two disagree, so an empty result means every rollup faithfully tracks the SoR.
`*_events`/`*_credits` are the two sides; a non-empty list flags a rollup that needs rebuilding
(e.g. a materialized view added after data already existed, or a merge/ingest anomaly).

actual.description: Actual spend to charge, as an exact decimal string.

SettleUsageBody.description:
`POST /v1/usage/reservations/{id}/settle` — price the actual usage against a catalog model and
settle the reservation. Idempotent on the reservation id.

rate_card_id.description: Optional synced rate-card id to price against; defaults to the catalog card for `model`.

SimulateBody.description:
`POST /v1/simulate` — re-rate a stream of usage events from one catalog model onto another to
preview the credit impact of switching, without touching the ledger.

Unit.description: The unit a component is priced in.

UsageDimensions.description: Token counts for a metered usage event.

tags.name: accounts
tags.description: Ledger accounts: open, balance, grants, credit-notes, entries

tags.name: events
tags.description: Usage events: record, batch, amend (append-only), void-run
